common.description: Ankora helps you smooth your bills, anticipate every invoice and stay in control of your budget. A clear, secure cockpit — no investment advice.

smoothing.title: Automatic smoothing
smoothing.body: Your annual and quarterly bills spread across 12 months. No more nasty surprises at year-end.

assistant.title: Transfer assistant
assistant.body: Every month, Ankora tells you exactly how much to transfer to your savings — and how much to keep in your current account for this month's bills.

secure.title: Isolated and secure
secure.body: Your data never leaves your private space. Encryption, RLS and audit logs by default. EU hosting.

one.title: Enter your bills
one.body: Rent, insurance, subscriptions… sorted by category and frequency.

two.title: Ankora smooths
two.body: Each annual bill is spread across 12 months to calculate your ideal reserve.

three.title: Steer your month
three.body: The cockpit tells you how much to transfer, how much to keep, and whether you're ahead or behind on your reserves.

advice.q: Is Ankora a financial advice tool?
advice.a: No. Ankora is a budgeting and financial education tool. It does not provide investment recommendations or personalised financial advice.

storage.a: Your data is hosted in the European Union (Supabase, EU region) with encryption at rest and strict per-user isolation via Row Level Security.

sharing.a: By default, your space is 100% private. Optional shared pockets (trips, joint projects) are coming in a future release — you'll explicitly choose what you share, and with whom.

faq.metaDescription: Frequently asked questions about Ankora: security, data, how it works, privacy.
faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Ankora before getting started.

bankConnection.q: Does Ankora connect to my bank?
bankConnection.a: No. Ankora does not use the PSD2 directive. You enter your bills and expenses manually. This choice guarantees you remain the owner of your data and that no sensitive banking information is stored.

dataLocation.a: All your data is hosted in the European Union (Supabase EU region, Vercel EU region, Upstash EU). No data is transferred outside the EU.

smoothing.a: Ankora takes your quarterly, semi-annual and annual bills and spreads them across 12 months. You know exactly how much to set aside each month to cover all your future bills, stress-free.

deletion.a: Yes, at any time from Settings → Privacy. Deletion takes effect 30 days after your request (you can cancel during this period). All your financial data is wiped, security logs are pseudonymised.

export.a: Yes. The "Download my data" button in Settings gives you a complete JSON file of everything Ankora holds about you. Compliant with the GDPR right to portability (art. 20).

advice.a: No. Ankora is a budgeting education and organisation tool. We do not issue any investment advice (FSMA Belgium regulatory constraint). For any investment decision, consult an authorised professional.

ai.a: In Phase 1, no. Calculations are deterministic and auditable. In Phase 2, an optional BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) layer will let you connect your own Anthropic or OpenRouter key for suggestions — no extra cost on Ankora's side.

sharing.q: Can I share my space with my children or friends?
sharing.a: In Phase 1, each user has a private space, isolated by default. In Phase 2, we'll add the ability to create "shared pockets" (by invitation, with viewer/editor roles) without ever mixing your personal data.

cgu.metaDescription: Ankora's terms of service — rules for using the service.
cgu.title: Terms of service

s1.heading: 1. Purpose
s1.body: Ankora is a budgeting and financial education tool for individuals. It lets you manually enter your bills and expenses, smooth them across the year, and anticipate cash-flow needs.

s2.heading: 2. What Ankora is NOT
s2.item1: Ankora is not an investment advisory service.
s2.item2: Ankora is not a banking service and holds no funds.
s2.item3: Ankora is not a PSD2 aggregator — no connection to your bank accounts.
s2.outro: The information displayed consists of estimates based on your inputs. It does not constitute personalised financial advice. For any important decision, consult an authorised professional.

s3.heading: 3. Account
s3.item1: You must be of legal age or have authorisation from a legal representative.
s3.item4: You are responsible for keeping your credentials confidential.

s4.heading: 4. Acceptable use
s4.item1: use the service for illegal purposes,
s4.item4: share your account with a third party.

s5.heading: 5. Intellectual property
s5.body: You remain the owner of your data. Ankora retains ownership of its code, brand, design and documentation.

s6.heading: 6. Liability
s6.body: Ankora is provided "as is". The publisher cannot be held liable for financial decisions made on the basis of the information displayed. No 100% availability is guaranteed.

s7.heading: 7. Termination
s7.body: You can delete your account at any time from Settings → Privacy. Deletion takes effect 30 days after the request (cancellable during that period).

s8.heading: 8. Governing law
s8.body: These terms are governed by Belgian law. Competent court: Brussels.

cgu.draft: Our terms of service are being drafted.
cgu.contact: For any questions, contact us: thierryvm@gmail.com

privacy.metaDescription: Ankora's privacy policy — EU hosting, GDPR, user rights.
privacy.title: Privacy policy

s1.heading: 1. Data controller
s1.body: Ankora, published by Thierry Van Mele (Belgium). Contact: <mail>thierryvm@gmail.com</mail>.

s2.heading: 2. Data collected
s2.intro: Ankora collects only the data strictly necessary to provide the service:
s2.item2: Financial data you enter: bills, expenses, categories, declared income, savings balance.
s2.item3: Technical data: IP address, user-agent (for security logs only).
s2.outro: Ankora does not connect to any bank (no PSD2 aggregation). You enter your bills and expenses yourself.

s4.heading: 4. Hosting and subprocessors
s4.outro: No data is transferred outside the EU/EEA.

s5.heading: 5. Retention period
s5.item1: Active account: for as long as you use the service.
s5.item2: Deleted account: deletion takes effect 30 days after your request (cancellable grace period).
s5.item3: Security logs: 12 months maximum, pseudonymised after account deletion.

s6.heading: 6. Your rights (GDPR art. 15-22)
s6.item1: Access: view your data at any time in your space.
s6.item2: Rectification: edit profile and data in the dedicated pages.
s6.item3: Erasure: "Delete my account" button in Settings → Privacy.
s6.item4: Portability: "Download my data" button — full JSON export.
s6.item5: Objection / Restriction: refuse or withdraw non-essential cookies at any time.

s7.heading: 7. Security
s7.item1: Encryption in transit (TLS 1.3) and at rest.
s7.item2: Supabase Row Level Security on all tables.
s7.item3: Rate limiting, strict CSP, append-only audit log.

s9.heading: 9. Changes
s9.body: Any material change to this policy is notified by email and requires your renewed consent to keep using the service.

privacy.draft: Our privacy policy is being drafted.
privacy.contact: For any questions, contact us: thierryvm@gmail.com

cookies.metaDescription: Ankora's cookie policy — full granularity, everything refusable except essentials.
cookies.title: Cookie policy
cookies.essentialBody: Required for the service to work: authentication session, theme preference, anti-CSRF.
cookies.analyticsBody1: Only if you give your consent. Aggregated usage measurement, with no personal identifier.
cookies.analyticsBody2: Ankora does not use Google Analytics. Vercel metrics are aggregated and anonymised.
cookies.marketingBody: Ankora currently uses no marketing cookies.
cookies.manageBody1: You can change your consents at any time in Settings → Privacy or by clicking "Manage cookies" in the footer.
cookies.manageBody2: Declining non-essential cookies does not degrade the experience: Ankora remains fully functional.
cookies.draft: This cookie policy page is being drafted.
cookies.contact: For any questions, contact us: thierryvm@gmail.com

offline.metaDescription: You are currently offline. Reconnect to access Ankora.
offline.title: You are offline
offline.message: Reconnect to access your cockpit. Your data stays intact.

consent.title: Cookies and privacy
consent.body: We only use essential cookies to log you in. Analytics cookies are off by default — you can turn them on if you want to help us improve Ankora. You can change your mind at any time via <link>the cookie policy</link>.

login.title: Log in
login.subtitle: Get back to your Ankora cockpit.

signup.passwordHint: At least 12 characters, 1 uppercase, 1 lowercase, 1 digit.
signup.googleTerms: By continuing with Google, you accept our <cgu>Terms</cgu> and <privacy>privacy policy</privacy>.

checkEmail.title: Check your inbox
checkEmail.body: We've just sent you a confirmation link. Click it to activate your account.

step1.title: Name your space
step1.description: You can change it later in settings.

step2.title: Your net monthly income
step2.description: Used as the basis for suggesting your monthly transfers.

dashboard.emptyDescription: Rent, insurance, subscriptions — Ankora smooths them over 12 months for you.
dashboard.planDescription: The transfers to run at the start of the month across your three accounts.
dashboard.missingSetupDescription: To calculate your Smart Transfer, Ankora needs your monthly income and the fixed amount you send to Daily Spending.
dashboard.transferPrincipalRemainingHint: After salary, transfers and Main bills ({bills}).

charges.title: My bills
charges.subtitle: Every bill is automatically smoothed over 12 months.
charges.count: {count, plural, =0 {no bills} one {1 bill} other {# bills}}

accounts.metaDescription: Track the balances of your Main, Daily Spending and Savings accounts. Set your monthly income and your transfer to Daily Spending.
accounts.title: My accounts
accounts.subtitle: Enter your real balances so Ankora can accurately calculate your smart transfer each month.

income.title: Net monthly income
income.description: The salary that lands every month in your Main account. Used to calculate what's left after bills and the transfer to Daily Spending.

transfer.title: Monthly transfer to Daily Spending
transfer.description: Fixed amount transferred every month from Main to your Daily Spending account. This is what covers groceries, fuel and dining.

principal.description: Receives the salary, pays fixed monthly bills.

expenses.title: My expenses
expenses.subtitle: Spending outside recurring bills — groceries, dining, leisure…
expenses.summary: {count, plural, =0 {No expenses} one {1 expense · {total}} other {# expenses · {total}}}
